title: Course Schedule
description:
There are `numCourses` courses labeled from `0` to `numCourses - 1`. You are given an array `prerequisites` where `prerequisites[i] = [a, b]` means you must take course `b` before course `a`.

Return `true` if you can finish all courses, or `false` if there is a cycle.

Example: `numCourses = 2`, `prerequisites = [[1,0]]` → `true` (take 0 then 1)
hints: This is cycle detection in a directed graph.
Build an adjacency list from prerequisites, then check for cycles using DFS.
BFS approach (Kahn's algorithm): track in-degrees, process nodes with in-degree 0.
